SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.configuration

Class ResilienceFailoverExecutionAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.configuration.ResilienceFailoverExecutionAutoConfiguration
@ConditionalOnExpression("${failover.enabled:true} eq true and \'${failover.type:basic}\'.toLowerCase() eq \'resilience\'") @ConditionalOnClass(name="io.github.resilience4j.circuitbreaker.CircuitBreaker") @AutoConfiguration(after=FailoverAutoConfiguration.class) public class ResilienceFailoverExecutionAutoConfiguration extends Object
Autoconfiguration for the Resilience4j-based FailoverExecution.

Activates when failover.type=resilience and the Resilience4j CircuitBreaker class is on the classpath. Registers a ResilienceFailoverExecution bean backed by the application's CircuitBreakerRegistry.

Warning: do not combine this with other resilience or retry mechanisms (Feign retry, Spring Retry, etc.) — stacking failover strategies causes unpredictable interaction and performance degradation.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Details

    • ResilienceFailoverExecutionAutoConfiguration

      public ResilienceFailoverExecutionAutoConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • failoverExecution

      @Bean public FailoverExecution<Object> failoverExecution(FailoverHandler<Object> failoverHandler, MethodExceptionHandler methodExceptionHandler, io.github.resilience4j.circuitbreaker.CircuitBreakerRegistry circuitBreakerRegistry)
      Registers ResilienceFailoverExecution backed by the application's CircuitBreakerRegistry.
      Parameters:
      failoverHandler - assembled failover handler
      methodExceptionHandler - exception handler applying the configured policy
      circuitBreakerRegistry - Resilience4j registry providing per-failover circuit breakers
      Returns:
      ResilienceFailoverExecution wrapping the handler with circuit-breaker protection
